rulings.Case Title: FLAVIA PINTO VS. KELVIN YEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 22, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF FLAVIA PINTO MOTION TO RELIEVE AS COUNSEL is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1013. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And For Leave To Sue Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 22, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF AE RETAIL WEST, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND FOR LEAVE TO SUE RECEIVER is continued to November 21, 2024. rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. ANIYAH KEITH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 22, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT is continued to November 1, 2024. No later than October 25, 2024 Plaintiff to file and serve a supplemental brief limited to citations to cases where Stipulation for Entry of Judgment (not an actual judgment) was enforced pursuant to CCP 128. CCP 128 is the only code section cited in the "Court Has Authority to Enter Judgment" section of the moving brief. If no such case authority is located, the brief shall state "no cases." No further argument shall be included in the brief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE T. LUI ET AL VS. GARY LUI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment For Possession Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 22, 2024 line 8. PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE LUI , AS CO-TRUSTEES OF THE LUI FAMILY TRUST DATED AUGUST 27, 2005, TERRY LUI, GLORIA LEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY is continued to October 31, 2024 for Plaintiff to provide authority for the proposition that a motion for summary judgment maybe filed on the same grounds as the previously denied motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff's supplemental brief shall be limited to citations to relevant authority; no additional argument. If no authorities on point found, the brief shall be limited to "No authorities." Brief shall be filed and served no later than October 25, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
